Group 1.title: How to upload coding files on GitHub
Group 1.text:
Summary
To create a new GitHub repository and upload your code, you initialize a local Git repository, link it to the remote GitHub repository, and then push your files. For future changes, you only need to add, commit, and push. 

Step-by-Step Procedure

1. Create a New Repository on GitHub
Go to github.com and log in.
Click the + icon and select "New repository". 
Name it (e.g., new-project), choose Public/Private, and do not initialize with a README. 
Click "Create repository".

2. Initialize and Push Your Local Project
Run these commands in your project's terminal:

git init
git add .
git commit -m "Initial commit"
git remote add origin https://github.com/Prateek-9801/new-project.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main

3. Upload Future Code Changes
For any new files or edits, run:

git add .
git commit -m "Describe your changes"
git push
